question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single GET /contracts/results/logs request with no timestamp filter, causing optimizeTimestampFilters() to invoke bindTimestampRange() which issues an additional DB query for the first transaction timestamp, followed by a full-table multi-union SQL scan on contract_log with no contractId constraint, resulting in sustained DB CPU and I/O spike exceeding 30% above baseline?
